attractions.name: Sapporo TV Tower
attractions.seo_title: Sapporo TV Tower — Panoramic Views Over Odori Park
attractions.seo_description: Visit Sapporo TV Tower for 360° views over Odori Park and central Sapporo. Tickets, hours, and best photo spots.
attractions.description_short: A 147-meter landmark tower at the east end of Odori Park with an observation deck over downtown Sapporo.
attractions.description_long: Opened in 1957, Sapporo TV Tower anchors the city's main boulevard and offers one of the best elevated views in Hokkaido. From the deck you can see Odori Park, the mountains beyond the city, and the seasonal festivals that fill the park below.

attractions.name: Sapporo Beer Museum
attractions.seo_title: Sapporo Beer Museum — Hokkaido's Brewing Heritage
attractions.seo_description: Explore the Sapporo Beer Museum in the historic red-brick brewery. Learn about Japan's oldest beer brand and tasting options.
attractions.description_short: Japan's only beer museum, housed in a red-brick brewery from the Meiji era.
attractions.description_long: The museum traces the story of beer in Japan and the founding of the Sapporo Brewery in 1876. Exhibits cover industrial heritage, Hokkaido's development, and the brewing process, with optional tastings of classic Sapporo beers.

attractions.name: Hokkaido Shrine
attractions.seo_title: Hokkaido Shrine — Sacred Forest in Maruyama Park
attractions.seo_description: Visit Hokkaido Shrine (Jingu) in Maruyama Park. A serene Shinto sanctuary surrounded by forest near central Sapporo.
attractions.description_short: The principal Shinto shrine of Hokkaido, set in a peaceful forest in Maruyama Park.
attractions.description_long: Established in 1869 to enshrine deities protecting Hokkaido's development, the shrine is especially popular during cherry blossom season and the New Year. The approach through Maruyama Park feels worlds away from the city center.

attractions.name: Former Hokkaido Government Office
attractions.seo_title: Former Hokkaido Government Office — Sapporo's Red Brick Landmark
attractions.seo_description: See the iconic red-brick Former Hokkaido Government Office. Neo-Baroque architecture and free exhibits in central Sapporo.
attractions.description_short: A striking red-brick Neo-Baroque building nicknamed Akarenga, symbol of Hokkaido's pioneer era.
attractions.description_long: Built in 1888, this American-influenced government building now houses galleries on Hokkaido's history and culture. Its rose gardens and symmetrical facade make it one of the most photographed landmarks in Sapporo.

attractions.name: Moerenuma Park
attractions.seo_title: Moerenuma Park — Isamu Noguchi's Sculptural Landscape
attractions.seo_description: Discover Moerenuma Park, a vast art park designed by Isamu Noguchi with geometric hills, fountains, and seasonal events.
attractions.description_short: A futuristic public park where sculpture and landscape merge on a former landfill site.
attractions.description_long: Designed by artist Isamu Noguchi and completed after his death, Moerenuma Park features massive play mountains, glass pyramids, and choreographed fountains. It is one of Hokkaido's most unique outdoor attractions for families and design lovers.
long_tail_keywords: Moerenuma Park how to get there

attractions.name: Mount Moiwa
attractions.seo_title: Mount Moiwa — Sapporo's Best Night View
attractions.seo_description: Ride the ropeway to Mount Moiwa for Sapporo's famous night panorama. One of Japan's top three night views.
attractions.description_short: A forested peak with a ropeway and observation deck famous for glittering city night views.
attractions.description_long: Mount Moiwa rises above western Sapporo and is ranked among Japan's finest night vistas. A ropeway and mini cable car bring visitors to the summit deck, where the city lights spread across the Ishikari Plain.

attractions.name: Nijo Market
attractions.seo_title: Nijo Market — Fresh Seafood and Hokkaido Street Food
attractions.seo_description: Eat your way through Nijo Market in Sapporo. Crab, sea urchin, melons, and local specialties in a compact downtown market.
attractions.description_short: A lively covered market serving Hokkaido's freshest seafood and produce since the early 1900s.
attractions.description_long: Nijo Market is where locals and chefs shop for crab, scallops, salmon roe, and seasonal Hokkaido produce. Many stalls grill seafood to order, making it an essential morning stop before exploring central Sapporo.

attractions.name: Maruyama Zoo
attractions.seo_title: Maruyama Zoo — Family Day Out in Sapporo
attractions.seo_description: Visit Maruyama Zoo next to Hokkaido Shrine. Polar bears, red pandas, and a compact layout ideal for families.
attractions.description_short: Hokkaido's oldest zoo, set beside Maruyama Park and the Hokkaido Shrine forest.
attractions.description_long: Opened in 1951, Maruyama Zoo is known for successful breeding programs and close-up habitats for polar bears, wolves, and red pandas. Its location next to Maruyama Park makes it easy to combine with shrine and cherry-blossom visits.
long_tail_keywords: things to do with kids Sapporo

attractions.name: Dazaifu Tenmangu
attractions.seo_title: Dazaifu Tenmangu — Shrine of Learning and Plum Blossoms
attractions.seo_description: Day trip to Dazaifu Tenmangu from Fukuoka. Historic shrine to scholar Sugawara no Michizane and famous plum garden.
attractions.description_short: Kyushu's most visited shrine, dedicated to the god of scholarship and adorned with thousands of plum trees.
attractions.description_long: Founded in 905 AD over the grave of scholar Sugawara no Michizane, Dazaifu Tenmangu draws students praying for exam success. The long approach lined with shops leads to vermilion halls, ponds, and a museum of treasures.
long_tail_keywords: how to get to Dazaifu from Fukuoka
best time for plum blossoms Dazaifu

attractions.name: Ohori Park
attractions.seo_title: Ohori Park — Fukuoka's Scenic Lake and Japanese Garden
attractions.seo_description: Stroll Ohori Park around its central lake, cross the causeway to the island, and visit the traditional Japanese garden.
attractions.description_short: A spacious city park centered on a large lake with jogging paths, boats, and a classic Japanese garden.
attractions.description_long: Once part of Fukuoka Castle's outer moat, Ohori Park is now the green heart of the city. A path circles the lake past pavilions and the Fukuoka Art Museum, while the attached Japanese garden offers tea-house views.

attractions.name: Fukuoka Castle
attractions.seo_title: Fukuoka Castle Ruins — Maizuru Park and Cherry Blossoms
attractions.seo_description: Explore Fukuoka Castle ruins in Maizuru Park. Stone walls, turrets, and one of Kyushu's best hanami spots.
attractions.description_short: Remains of a mighty hilltop castle in Maizuru Park, famous for spring cherry blossoms.
attractions.description_long: Built for lord Kuroda Nagamasa in the early 1600s, Fukuoka Castle once dominated the city. Today its stone ramparts and reconstructed towers sit within Maizuru Park, a favorite for cherry-blossom viewing and history walks.

attractions.name: Kushida Shrine
attractions.seo_title: Kushida Shrine — Heart of Hakata and the Yamakasa Festival
attractions.seo_description: Visit Kushida Shrine in Hakata, spiritual home of Fukuoka's famous Yamakasa summer festival.
attractions.description_short: An ancient Shinto shrine in Hakata that hosts the spectacular Hakata Gion Yamakasa festival each July.
attractions.description_long: Dating to 757 AD, Kushida Shrine anchors Hakata's old merchant quarter. Its decorated floats and ritual races during Yamakasa define Fukuoka's summer, while year-round visitors come for luck charms and the sacred ginkgo tree.

attractions.name: Canal City Hakata
attractions.seo_title: Canal City Hakata — Shopping, Dining, and Fountain Shows
attractions.seo_description: Explore Canal City Hakata, Fukuoka's canal-side entertainment complex with shops, ramen, and water shows.
attractions.description_short: A colorful waterfront mall where canals, fountains, and theaters create a mini city within the city.
attractions.description_long: Opened in 1996, Canal City Hakata combines retail, cinemas, hotels, and restaurants along artificial canals. Evening fountain light shows and a concentration of ramen shops make it a popular evening destination near Kushida Shrine.

attractions.name: Fukuoka Tower
attractions.seo_title: Fukuoka Tower — Seaside Observation on Momochi Beach
attractions.seo_description: Visit Fukuoka Tower on Momochi Seaside Park for 234-meter-high views over Hakata Bay and the city skyline.
attractions.description_short: Japan's tallest seaside tower, covered in mirrored glass and overlooking Momochi Beach.
attractions.description_long: Standing 234 meters above Momochi Seaside Park, Fukuoka Tower offers sweeping views of Hakata Bay, Nokonoshima Island, and on clear days the mountains of Kyushu. Its triangular profile has become a symbol of modern Fukuoka.

attractions.name: Nanzoin Temple
attractions.seo_title: Nanzoin Temple — Giant Reclining Buddha of Sasaguri
attractions.seo_description: See the massive bronze Reclining Buddha at Nanzoin Temple near Fukuoka, one of the largest bronze statues in the world.
attractions.description_short: A hillside temple famous for a 41-meter-long bronze Reclining Buddha surrounded by forest.
attractions.description_long: Nanzoin Temple in Sasaguri is home to one of the world's largest bronze reclining Buddha images. The statue lies along a forested path dotted with smaller shrines, offering a peaceful contrast to central Fukuoka's bustle.
long_tail_keywords: how to get to Nanzoin from Fukuoka

attractions.name: Momochi Seaside Park
attractions.seo_title: Momochi Seaside Park — Beach, Tower, and Bay Views
attractions.seo_description: Relax at Momochi Seaside Park on Hakata Bay. Urban beach, Fukuoka Tower, and waterfront walks in western Fukuoka.
attractions.description_short: An artificial beach and waterfront park at the foot of Fukuoka Tower along Hakata Bay.
attractions.description_long: Momochi Seaside Park brings a resort atmosphere to Fukuoka with a sandy beach, marinas, and open lawns. It is the setting for summer festivals, fireworks, and sunset views beside the mirrored Fukuoka Tower.

attractions.name: Shofukuji Temple
attractions.seo_title: Shofukuji Temple — Japan's First Zen Temple
attractions.seo_description: Visit Shofukuji in Hakata, the first Zen temple founded in Japan. Quiet courtyards minutes from Hakata Station.
attractions.description_short: Founded in 1195, the oldest Zen temple in Japan with serene moss gardens and wooden halls.
attractions.description_long: Myōan Eisai founded Shofukuji after returning from China, establishing Rinzai Zen in Japan. Although halls are not open for entry, visitors can walk the paths between gates and admire the contemplative architecture of old Hakata.

attractions.name: Tochoji Temple
attractions.seo_title: Tochoji Temple — Giant Wooden Buddha in Hakata
attractions.seo_description: See the 10.8-meter seated wooden Buddha at Tochoji Temple, a hidden gem near Hakata Station in Fukuoka.
attractions.description_short: A Shingon temple housing one of Japan's largest wooden seated Buddha statues.
attractions.description_long: Founded by Kukai in 806, Tochoji was the family temple of the Kuroda lords of Fukuoka. Its five-story pagoda and the towering wooden Daibutsu in the main hall are surprises just minutes from Hakata Station.

attractions.name: Kenroku-en
attractions.seo_title: Kenroku-en — One of Japan's Three Great Gardens
attractions.seo_description: Walk Kenroku-en in Kanazawa, a masterpiece of landscape design with ponds, teahouses, and seasonal beauty.
attractions.description_short: A strolling garden ranked among Japan's three finest, beside Kanazawa Castle.
attractions.description_long: Developed over centuries by the Maeda clan, Kenroku-en embodies six classic attributes of Chinese garden theory. Cherry blossoms, irises, and snow-draped pines make it stunning in every season next to Kanazawa Castle.

attractions.name: Kanazawa Castle
attractions.seo_title: Kanazawa Castle — Restored Halls and Lead-Tiled Roofs
attractions.seo_description: Tour Kanazawa Castle's reconstructed gates and the distinctive lead-tiled Ishikawa-mon gate beside Kenroku-en.
attractions.description_short: Seat of the powerful Maeda clan, with striking lead-covered roof tiles and restored wooden gates.
attractions.description_long: Kanazawa Castle burned repeatedly but its massive stone walls and iconic lead-tiled roofs remain. Recent reconstructions of the Hishi-yagura and Gojikken-nagaya storehouses let visitors appreciate samurai-era craftsmanship.

attractions.name: Higashi Chaya District
attractions.seo_title: Higashi Chaya District — Kanazawa's Geisha Quarter
attractions.seo_description: Stroll Higashi Chaya, Kanazawa's best-preserved teahouse district with wooden machiya and gold-leaf crafts.
attractions.description_short: A historic street of ochaya teahouses where geisha once entertained merchants and samurai.
attractions.description_long: Higashi Chaya's lattice-fronted machiya date to the Edo period when Kanazawa rivaled Kyoto in culture. Today you can visit gold-leaf workshops, sip matcha in teahouses, and experience shamisen performances in the evening.

attractions.name: Nagamachi Samurai District
attractions.seo_title: Nagamachi — Samurai Residences and Earthen Walls
attractions.seo_description: Explore Nagamachi, Kanazawa's samurai quarter with mud walls, Nomura-ke House, and narrow stone lanes.
attractions.description_short: Former samurai neighborhood with earthen walls, canals, and restored warrior residences.
attractions.description_long: Nagamachi preserves the atmosphere of a feudal retainers' quarter. The Nomura-ke samurai house displays exquisite gardens and armor, while mud-walled lanes and canals evoke the Edo-period city of the Maeda lords.

attractions.name: 21st Century Museum of Contemporary Art
attractions.seo_title: 21st Century Museum — Contemporary Art in Kanazawa
attractions.seo_description: Visit Kanazawa's circular contemporary art museum. Leandro Erlich's Swimming Pool and rotating world-class exhibitions.
attractions.description_short: An award-winning circular museum blending interactive contemporary art with public space.
attractions.description_long: Designed by SANAA architects, this museum has no formal facade — visitors enter from all sides into a glass cylinder. Permanent favorites include Leandro Erlich's illusion Swimming Pool and James Turrell light installations.

attractions.name: Omicho Market
attractions.seo_title: Omicho Market — Kanazawa's Kitchen by the Sea
attractions.seo_description: Eat fresh seafood at Omicho Market, Kanazawa's bustling covered market near the castle since the Edo period.
attractions.description_short: A lively market called Kanazawa's Kitchen for crab, sashimi, and Sea of Japan specialties.
attractions.description_long: For over 280 years Omicho Market has supplied Kanazawa with fish from the Sea of Japan. Narrow aisles brim with stalls selling snow crab, amberjack, and local vegetables — best visited in the morning for breakfast bowls.

attractions.name: Myoryuji Temple
attractions.seo_title: Myoryuji (Ninja Temple) — Secret Passages and Traps
attractions.seo_description: Tour Myoryuji, the Ninja Temple in Kanazawa with hidden stairs, trapdoors, and feudal-era defenses.
attractions.description_short: A temple nicknamed Ninja Temple for its maze of secret tunnels, traps, and hidden rooms.
attractions.description_long: Built by the Maeda clan as a disguised military outpost, Myoryuji looks like an ordinary two-story temple but conceals four floors of passages, suicide pits, and observation points. Guided tours reveal its ingenious Edo-period defenses.

attractions.name: D.T. Suzuki Museum
attractions.seo_title: D.T. Suzuki Museum — Zen Philosophy in Architecture
attractions.seo_description: Reflect at the D.T. Suzuki Museum, a minimalist space honoring the Zen scholar who brought Buddhism to the West.
attractions.description_short: A contemplative museum dedicated to Zen philosopher D.T. Suzuki, with water gardens and clean lines.
attractions.description_long: Opened in 2011 near Kenroku-en, this museum celebrates Suzuki's role introducing Zen to global audiences. Architect Taniguchi Yoshio's design uses light, water, and empty space to encourage quiet meditation.

attractions.name: Oyama Shrine
attractions.seo_title: Oyama Shrine — Dutch-Influenced Gate in Kanazawa
attractions.seo_description: See Oyama Shrine's unique Shinmon gate blending Japanese, Chinese, and Dutch architectural styles.
attractions.description_short: A shrine known for its eclectic gate that once held a Dutch-style stained-glass lantern on top.
attractions.description_long: Oyama Shrine honors Maeda Toshiie and his wife Matsu. Its three-story Shinmon gate combines Buddhist, Shinto, and European elements — a rare symbol of Kanazawa's openness to Dutch trade during the Edo period.

attractions.name: Kazuemachi Chaya District
attractions.seo_title: Kazuemachi Chaya — Quiet Teahouses by the Asano River
attractions.seo_description: Discover Kazuemachi, a smaller teahouse district along the Asano River with stone steps and evening lanterns.
attractions.description_short: A lesser-known geisha district on the Asano River, quieter than Higashi Chaya but equally atmospheric.
attractions.description_long: Kazuemachi's stone-paved slope descends to the Asano River past wooden teahouses lit by paper lanterns. It offers a more intimate alternative to Higashi Chaya, especially romantic at dusk when reflections shimmer on the water.

attractions.name: Kobe Harborland
attractions.seo_title: Kobe Harborland — Waterfront Shopping and Night Views
attractions.seo_description: Explore Kobe Harborland's malls, ferris wheel, and bay views. A classic evening stroll in Kobe.
attractions.description_short: A bayside shopping and entertainment district with illuminated arcades and harbor views.
attractions.description_long: Harborland stretches along Kobe's port with shopping centers, the Mosaic complex, and an observation ferris wheel. At night the waterfront glows against the Rokko mountains, making it one of Kansai's most romantic urban promenades.

attractions.name: Mount Rokko
attractions.seo_title: Mount Rokko — Panoramic Views Over Kobe and Osaka Bay
attractions.seo_description: Take the ropeway to Mount Rokko for million-dollar views over Kobe, Osaka, and Awaji Island on clear days.
attractions.description_short: A mountain ridge behind Kobe famous for night views, alpine plants, and cool summer retreats.
attractions.description_long: Mount Rokko rises to 931 meters and offers one of Japan's legendary night panoramas. Ropeways and a historic cable car connect the city to gardens, an alpine botanical garden, and the Rokko Garden Terrace viewpoint.

attractions.name: Nunobiki Falls
attractions.seo_title: Nunobiki Falls — Sacred Waterfalls Above Kobe
attractions.seo_description: Hike to Nunobiki Falls minutes from Shin-Kobe Station. A series of waterfalls revered since ancient times.
attractions.description_short: A dramatic cascade of four waterfalls in the hills behind Shin-Kobe Station.
attractions.description_long: Nunobiki is considered one of Japan's greatest waterfalls and appears in Heian-period literature. A well-maintained trail climbs through forest past the successive falls to viewpoints over Kobe and the port below.

attractions.name: Ikuta Shrine
attractions.seo_title: Ikuta Shrine — Ancient Sanctuary in Modern Kobe
attractions.seo_description: Visit Ikuta Shrine, one of Japan's oldest shrines, tucked between Sannomiya's skyscrapers in central Kobe.
attractions.description_short: A Shinto shrine dating to the 3rd century, surrounded by downtown Kobe's shopping streets.
attractions.description_long: Ikuta Shrine predates the city itself and enshrines the goddess Wakahirume. Despite wars and the 1995 earthquake, its forested precinct remains an oasis steps from Sannomiya Station in the heart of modern Kobe.

attractions.name: Kobe Port Tower
attractions.seo_title: Kobe Port Tower — Iconic Red Lattice Harbor Landmark
attractions.seo_description: Visit Kobe Port Tower in Meriken Park for harbor views inside its distinctive red hyperboloid structure.
attractions.description_short: A 108-meter red steel tower shaped like a Japanese drum, symbol of Kobe's port.
attractions.description_long: Opened in 1963, Kobe Port Tower's lattice design was revolutionary and survived the 1995 earthquake. Its observation floors rotate for 360° views of the harbor, Rokko mountains, and Awaji Island.

attractions.name: Nankinmachi
attractions.seo_title: Nankinmachi — Kobe's Vibrant Chinatown
attractions.seo_description: Eat and shop in Nankinmachi, one of Japan's three major Chinatowns, famous for pork buns and street food.
attractions.description_short: Kobe's compact Chinatown near Motomachi, packed with food stalls and festival atmosphere.
attractions.description_long: Founded by Chinese merchants who opened Kobe's port, Nankinmachi bursts with color during Lunar New Year. Steamed pork buns, Peking duck wraps, and lantern-lined alleys make it a favorite quick stop before Harborland.

attractions.name: Arima Onsen
attractions.seo_title: Arima Onsen — Japan's Oldest Hot Spring Town
attractions.seo_description: Day trip to Arima Onsen from Kobe. Famous gold and silver waters in Japan's oldest recorded hot spring resort.
attractions.description_short: A historic mountain onsen town with famous golden and silver thermal waters.
attractions.description_long: Arima Onsen appears in ancient chronicles as one of Japan's three oldest hot springs. Nestled in the Rokko mountains, its narrow streets offer public baths, ryokan stays, and the unique iron-rich gold and clear silver waters.

attractions.name: Meriken Park
attractions.seo_title: Meriken Park — Harbor Promenade and Earthquake Memorial
attractions.seo_description: Walk Meriken Park along Kobe's port. Home to Port Tower, BE Kobe sculpture, and the 1995 earthquake memorial.
attractions.description_short: A waterfront park with modern art, the Port Tower, and memorials to the 1995 Great Hanshin Earthquake.
attractions.description_long: Meriken Park stretches along the harbor between Harborland and the port. The BE Kobe sculpture, maritime museum, and preserved quake-damaged waterfront sections tell the story of a city rebuilt after disaster.

attractions.name: Kitano-cho
attractions.seo_title: Kitano-cho — Western Residences and Ijinkan Houses
attractions.seo_description: Tour Kitano-cho's ijinkan foreign merchant houses on the slopes above Sannomiya in Kobe.
attractions.description_short: A hillside district of ornate Western-style mansions opened when Kobe became an international port.
attractions.description_long: After 1868 foreign merchants built stately ijinkan houses in Kitano-cho. Weathercock House and other residences are open as museums, blending European architecture with Japanese gardens and views toward the port.

attractions.name: Kobe Earthquake Memorial Museum
attractions.seo_title: Kobe Earthquake Memorial — Lessons of the 1995 Hanshin Disaster
attractions.seo_description: Visit the Great Hanshin-Awaji Earthquake Memorial in Kobe. Powerful exhibits on the 1995 quake and disaster preparedness.
attractions.description_short: A moving museum documenting the 1995 Great Hanshin Earthquake and Kobe's recovery.
attractions.description_long: The Disaster Reduction and Human Renewal Institution uses film, artifacts, and survivor testimonies to convey the January 1995 earthquake that devastated Kobe. It is both a memorial and an educational center for earthquake preparedness.

adventures.name: Mount Fuji
adventures.seo_title: Mount Fuji — Japan's Sacred Summit and Iconic Views
adventures.seo_description: Plan a Mount Fuji trip: climbing season, lakes, viewpoints, and day trips from Tokyo.
adventures.description_short: Japan's highest peak and most recognizable symbol, a UNESCO World Heritage cultural site.
adventures.description_long: Mount Fuji has inspired art and pilgrimage for centuries. Visitors can admire it from the Fuji Five Lakes, climb during the official summer season, or soak in nearby hot springs with the volcano on the horizon.
long_tail_keywords: day trip Mount Fuji from Tokyo

adventures.name: Hakone
adventures.seo_title: Hakone — Onsen, Lake Ashi, and Fuji Views
adventures.seo_description: Explore Hakone's hot springs, pirate ships on Lake Ashi, and ropeways with views of Mount Fuji.
adventures.description_short: A classic hot-spring region with lakes, volcanic valleys, and art museums near Tokyo.
adventures.description_long: Hakone's loop route combines cable cars, a scenic railway, and lake cruises past torii gates in the water. Onsen ryokan, the Hakone Open-Air Museum, and clear-day Fuji views make it a staple nature escape from Tokyo.

adventures.name: Nikko National Park
adventures.seo_title: Nikko — Shrines, Waterfalls, and Mountain Forests
adventures.seo_description: Day trip to Nikko for UNESCO shrines, Kegon Falls, and mountain scenery north of Tokyo.
adventures.description_short: A mountain region of lavish shrines, waterfalls, and hiking in Tochigi Prefecture.
adventures.description_long: Nikko's Toshogu Shrine honors Tokugawa Ieyasu with gold-leaf carvings and famous see no evil monkeys. Beyond the shrines, Lake Chuzenji and Kegon Falls offer cool alpine air and autumn foliage.

adventures.name: Kamikochi
adventures.seo_title: Kamikochi — Alpine Valley in the Japanese Alps
adventures.seo_description: Hike Kamikochi in the Northern Japan Alps. Crystal rivers, wooden Kappa Bridge, and mountain peaks.
adventures.description_short: A highland valley in the Japanese Alps with pristine rivers and dramatic peak views.
adventures.description_long: Kamikochi is accessible only by bus or taxi to protect its environment. Easy riverside trails lead from the iconic Kappa Bridge to Myojin Pond, with Hotaka peaks reflected in still water — a highlight of any Japan nature road trip.

adventures.name: Shirakawa-go
adventures.seo_title: Shirakawa-go — Gassho Farmhouses and Snowy Valleys
adventures.seo_description: Visit Shirakawa-go's UNESCO gassho-zukuri thatched villages in the Japanese Alps.
adventures.description_short: A UNESCO village of steep thatched roofs built to survive heavy mountain snow.
adventures.description_long: Shirakawa-go's gassho-style farmhouses cluster in a remote valley unchanged for centuries. Winter illuminations and rice-planting seasons draw photographers, while minshuku stays offer immersion in rural Japan.

adventures.name: Miyajima Island
adventures.seo_title: Miyajima — Floating Torii Gate and Itsukushima Shrine
adventures.seo_description: Ferry to Miyajima for the iconic floating torii, deer, and mountain trails near Hiroshima.
adventures.description_short: Sacred island famous for a vermilion torii gate rising from the Seto Inland Sea.
adventures.description_long: Itsukushima Shrine's torii appears to float at high tide, one of Japan's most photographed scenes. Friendly deer roam the village, and a ropeway climbs Mount Misen for panoramic island views.

adventures.name: Izu Peninsula
adventures.seo_title: Izu Peninsula — Coastal Hot Springs and Ocean Drives
adventures.seo_description: Road trip the Izu Peninsula for onsen towns, rugged coastlines, and views of Mount Fuji across the bay.
adventures.description_short: A volcanic peninsula south of Tokyo with hot springs, beaches, and scenic coastal roads.
adventures.description_long: Izu combines Pacific surf, cliffside temples, and famous onsen towns like Shuzenji. The Jogasaki Coast's suspension bridge and open-air baths overlooking the ocean make it ideal for a multi-day driving itinerary.

adventures.name: Yakushima
adventures.seo_title: Yakushima — Ancient Cedars and Rainforest Hiking
adventures.seo_description: Explore Yakushima's mossy forests and thousand-year-old cedars that inspired Studio Ghibli.
adventures.description_short: A subtropical island UNESCO site with ancient yakusugi cedar forests and heavy rainfall.
adventures.description_long: Yakushima's interior receives some of Japan's highest rainfall, nurturing lush moss forests and Jomon Sugi — a cedar estimated over 2,000 years old. Multi-day hikes and coastal onsen reward adventurous travelers.

adventures.name: Kumano Kodo
adventures.seo_description: Walk the Kumano Kodo pilgrimage routes to sacred shrines in the mountains of Wakayama.
adventures.description_short: UNESCO pilgrimage paths through cedar forests linking grand shrines of the Kumano region.
adventures.description_long: For over a thousand years emperors and commoners have walked these mountain trails to Kumano Sanzan shrines. Stone-paved paths, village lodgings, and forest solitude offer a spiritual counterpoint to Japan's cities.

adventures.name: Jigokudani Monkey Park
adventures.seo_title: Jigokudani — Snow Monkeys in Hot Springs
adventures.seo_description: See wild Japanese macaques bathing in hot springs at Jigokudani Monkey Park in Nagano.
adventures.description_short: A valley park where wild snow monkeys soak in natural thermal pools during winter.
adventures.description_long: In the mountains of Nagano Prefecture, a troop of Japanese macaques descends to outdoor onsen pools when snow covers the valley. A short forest walk leads to the observation area for this uniquely Japanese wildlife experience.
